overall.summary: Overall score uses minimum dimension score 7 capped by highest severity MEDIUM.

proposal_semantics.proposal_summary: This proposal aims to fix the cross-chain governance bridge for Uniswap on Arbitrum. It involves temporarily disabling address-aliasing for the L1 Timelock, calling setOwner on the L2 Uniswap Factory to set the owner to the properly aliased L1 Timelock address, and then re-enabling aliasing.

disclosed_entities.name: L1 Uniswap Timelock contract
disclosed_entities.textual_evidence: ...disable address-aliasing for messages from the L1 TimeLock contract, 0x1a9c8182c09f50c8318d769245bea52c32be35bc.

disclosed_entities.name: L2 Uniswap factory
disclosed_entities.textual_evidence: The Uniswap Factory was deployed with the owner set to the same, unaliased address of the L1 Uniswap Timelock contract. To verify, see: owner address... set at https://arbiscan.io/address/0x1F98431c8aD98523631AE4a59f267346ea31F984#readContract

disclosed_entities.name: L1 Timelock’s address alias
disclosed_entities.textual_evidence: ...setting the owner to the L1 Timelock’s address alias, 0x2BAD8182C09F50c8318d769245beA52C32Be46CD.

disclosed_entities.name: Governance deployer key
disclosed_entities.textual_evidence: The governance deployer key on Arbitrum has burned the nonce... verifiable here: https://arbiscan.io/address/0x41653c7d61609d856f29355e404f310ec4142cfb

claimed_actions.raw_action: Disable address-aliasing for messages from the L1 TimeLock contract
claimed_actions.claimed_effect: Allows messages from L1 Timelock to preserve original L1 sender address
claimed_actions.textual_evidence: Arbitrum will add a temporary exception to disable address-aliasing for messages from the L1 TimeLock contract

claimed_actions.raw_action: Send cross-chain message to call setOwner on L2 Uniswap factory
claimed_actions.claimed_effect: Updates the owner of the L2 Uniswap Factory to the aliased L1 Timelock
claimed_actions.textual_evidence: the Uniswap governance contract will send a cross-chain message from the L1 Timelock contract to call setOwner on the L2 Uniswap factory, setting the owner to the L1 Timelock’s address alias

claimed_actions.claimed_effect: Restores normal cross-chain bridge behavior for L1 Timelock
claimed_actions.textual_evidence: Arbitrum will re-enable aliasing for the L1 Timelock contract address.

claimed_complexity.reason: The proposal requires coordinated off-chain actions by the Arbitrum team to temporarily modify bridge behavior, alongside an on-chain cross-chain call.
claimed_complexity.textual_evidence: The system governing the Arbitrum deployment was mis-configured... will require more involved intervention to repair. ... Arbitrum will add a temporary exception to disable address-aliasing... Once the final on-chain governance vote passes...

disclosed_functions.textual_evidence: call setOwner on the L2 Uniswap factory

limitations: The exact implementation details of how Arbitrum disables/re-enables aliasing are not specified.
No target address or signature provided for the governance execution steps.
